body: Live-test attempt on SLS(68)/WARRANT#54-60: EA correctly refused to start -- AutoTrading was off in the terminal (FAIL [01], cascading to FAIL [09] order test, error 4109), initialization failed within 10s, not a TUNGSTEN bug. Terminal fully closed since. Built permanent tooling so this never needs rediscovering: C:\Users\User\tungsten-ops\tungsten-attach-and-watch.ps1 (one script: swaps chart02.chr, launches terminal, watches all 3 logs with baselines captured BEFORE launch so nothing can be silently missed, flags init-failure as an unmissable line) plus a permanent path/gotcha reference added to the mt4_ea_swap_automation memory. To actually run it next: enable AutoTrading (Ctrl+E) before launch -- that's a manual step, no scriptable way found. Also ran a second forge-cleanup-3 audit round (systematic, not a repeat): checked every threshold-space comparison site and every weight-array-ordering site in the file. Both came back clean -- WARRANT#60 was the only instance of the threshold-space bug, and weight ordering is consistent everywhere. One already-self-flagged gap noted (Stage -1 localisation's simplified proxy scorer, SLS19-TODO CRITICAL1) but left alone -- bounded impact, real fix is a bigger refactor than fits a quick patch. No new WARRANT this round; a clean audit is the honest result.
